steps.title: Set up the two views
steps.content: Political scientists predict ads should reduce the knowledge gap. Cognitive scientists predict prior knowledge helps absorb new info — so high-knowledge viewers should benefit more, widening the gap.

steps.title: Apply the graph to the choices
steps.content:
If the ads produced an effect contrary to political scientists' prediction (gap didn't shrink), and that result is consistent with the cognitive-science explanation, choice B fits. A and D claim the political-science view was supported; C says "worsened" the problem but denies the cognitive phenomenon — internally inconsistent.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
